summary:
The patient has constitutional growth delay due to delayed skeletal maturation consistent with family history of short stature. Catch-up growth typically follows over time as she reaches typical adolescent milestones.

The patient likely has constitutional growth delay given her delayed bone age and lack of significant pubertal progression beyond early stages, which aligns with expected patterns seen in families with similar histories. This condition usually results in spontaneous catch-up growth leading toward an average adult height once full maturity is reached. 

Final Answer:

\boxed{\text{Constitutional Growth Delay}}
